title: Great sound for the price
text: The 4th gen really is noticeably better than the 3rd gen for sound quality. I have one in my kitchen and one in the bathroom. The sound fills a small to medium room well. For music listening it is not going to replace a dedicated speaker but for the kitchen while cooking it is perfect. Alexa response time is fast.

title: Privacy concerns but works as advertised
text: I wrestle with having an always-on microphone in my home but the functionality is undeniable. I use it primarily for timers, music, and smart home control. The smart home hub feature is genuinely useful — I have it controlling 6 Philips Hue bulbs. Does what it says.

title: Better sound than expected
text: I bought this primarily as a smart home hub and expected the audio quality to be poor. I was wrong. The bass is decent for a speaker this small. I use it for podcasts and background music while working. The spherical design looks cleaner than the old hockey puck shape.

title: Alexa keeps mishearing commands
text: The hardware is fine — solid build, decent sound. But Alexa mishears commands about 20% of the time in my experience. I have to repeat myself often. This may be a placement issue with my specific room but it is frustrating. The wake word sensitivity is hard to calibrate.

title: Good for elderly parent
text: I set one of these up for my 78-year-old mother who has difficulty with smartphones. She uses it to play her radio station, set reminders for medication, and call me. The setup was straightforward and Amazon's support was helpful when she called them directly. Exactly what I needed.

title: Useful for a home office
text: I use mine for quick unit conversions, timers while on calls, playing focus music, and hands-free calendar lookups. The far-field microphones pick up my voice clearly even when I am across the room. The spherical design sits unobtrusively on a desk.
